t_ch: …我明白你的意思，莫忒丝。但是命运三相殿的神谕绝不能传递绝望。你知道，这是为了雅努萨波利斯。
t_en: ...I understand what you are saying, Mortis. However, the Temple of the Three Fates cannot spread despair. You know that I must act for the sake of Janusopolis.

t_ch: 不过，你竟还念及我们的往日交情…多么令人意外啊。
t_en: Although, the fact that you still consider our former friendship... I must admit I am surprised.

t_ch: 答应你的密道完工在即。若有朝一日他们发难，你尽可带着孩子逃走，我会暗中掩护。
t_en: Work on the secret tunnel that I promised you is almost complete. If they attack soon, you will be able to escape with your children. I will cover you from the shadows.

t_ch: 只是…要尽早。你知道，我这年纪…已经看得见塞纳托斯的影子了。
t_en: It's just... It must be soon. As you know, I am not a young man anymore... I can already see Thanatos' shadow before me.

t_ch: …愿雅努斯揭示你的路径，柯德克斯。
t_en: ...May Janus guide your path, Codex.

t_ch: 「切莫假托神谕之名，给人以虚假的希望」吗……
t_en: "Do not give people false hope under the guise of prophecy...?"

t_ch: 呵…也许连我自己，都做不到吧……
t_en: Hah... Perhaps that is something that would be beyond even me...

t_ch: 哎，刚才那个往神殿走的人，衣衫褴褛的，你猜猜他是谁？
t_en: Hey, that person in the rags headed towards the temple just now. Guess who that was.

t_ch: 看他那服饰，挺面生的，估计是哪个逃荒至此的难民吧。
t_en: Judging by their clothes and the fact that I don't really recognize them... Probably a refugee seeking solace here?

t_ch: 这人是布利萨的国王，曾经的一城之主！
t_en: That man is the King of Bulsa, former lord of the city!

t_ch: 啊？怎会如此？他瘦骨嶙峋的，刚才在桥上都差点叫风刮到山崖底下，哪有半分国王的样子？
t_en: Huh? How can that be? He's skin and bones! The breeze on the bridge almost blew him down into the ravine just now. They don't look anything like a king?

t_ch: 唉…信仰艾格勒的布利萨，是个微风和煦的城邦。
t_en: Ah... Bulsa that worships Aquila, the city of gentle breeze.

t_ch: 尼卡多利和艾格勒的战争旷日持久…听说布利萨人早就和悬锋人结怨了。
t_en: The battle between Nikador and Aquila waged for many years... I hear that the people of Bulsa have a long-held grudge against the Kremnoans.

t_ch: 那你是怎么认识他的？
t_en: How do you know him then?

t_ch: 这国王几年前就来过雅努萨波利斯，那会儿你还没上任呢。
t_en: That king came to Janusopolis a few years ago, before you had taken office.

t_ch: 他知道布利萨不是悬锋城的对手，遂来求取能够拯救城邦的神谕。辅祭大人告诉他要善待并集结诸多盟邦，如此方能挫败天谴之锋的锐意。
t_en: He knew that Bulsa was no match for Castrum Kremnos and came in search of a prophecy to save his city. Sir Acolyte told him that if he gathers many allies and treats them well, he will be able to overcome the sharp edge of the Blade of Fury.

t_ch: 布利萨王深信不疑，回乡后就散尽国财，拉拢了附近不少小城邦。
t_en: The King of Bulsa trusted every word, returned to his city, and distributed his wealth to win over many small neighboring cities.

t_ch: 他本以为从此能高枕无忧，谁知悬锋城降临的时候，所有曾受他恩惠的小城邦畏惧天谴的锋芒，争先恐后地俯首称臣。
t_en: The King was convinced that he now had nothing to worry about. Who could have imagined that when Castrum Kremnos did attack, all of the small neighboring cities that had received his blessings were so scared of the Blade of Fury that they all rushed to surrender?

t_ch: 他们甚至还将先前布利萨王赠予的财物如数吐出，奉与悬锋人以示忠诚。
t_en: They even coughed up all of the gifts that the King of Bulsa had given them and offered them to the Kremnoans as a sign of their loyalty.

t_ch: 布利萨城不日便沦陷了。可怜布利萨王，也算是个忧国忧民的城主，如今却落得如此境地……
t_en: The city of Bulsa fell in days. The poor king of Bulsa... To see a man that cared for his city and people fall so far...

t_ch: 这还不是因为他盲信所谓的神谕……
t_en: Well, that's all because of his blind faith in the prophecy, isn't it...?

t_ch: 嘘，嘘！你想被记进圣女卫队不当言论册吗？站好你的岗吧。
t_en: Shh, shh! Do you want the Holy Maiden Guards to add you to the list of slanderous remarks? Go back to your post.

t_ch: 好在像布利萨那样牵涉进泰坦战争的城邦还不算多。希望和平能在翁法罗斯的大部分地方一直持续下去吧……
t_en: At least there weren't too many cities that were dragged into the strife of the Titans like Bulsa was. Let us hope that these days of peace will remain across the majority of Amphoreus...

t_ch: 哎，快看，他出来了！
t_en: Ah, look! He's back!

t_ch: 怎么样怎么样，波塔莫斯，快告诉我们，这里头是怎样的光景？
t_en: Well? Tell us, tell us, Potamos! What is it like in there?

t_ch: 传言是真的…好美的一座庭院，到处是闪闪发亮的小物件，萤火虫、玻璃片、彩纸…我从没见过这么漂亮的地方！
t_en: The rumors are true... It is a beautiful courtyard covered in shining objects: Fireflies, shards of glass, colored paper... I have never seen such a beautiful place!

t_ch: 那…那位医师小姑娘，她是怎么治病的？
t_en: So... So the young girl doctor... How is it that she cures people?

t_ch: 你们也知道，我与远在他邦的挚友失联很久，前几日才知道那座城邦毁于黑潮了。
t_en: As you all know, I lost contact with my dear friends in other cities long ago and only recently learned of their cities being devoured by the black tide.

t_ch: 身处这般末世，本来就压力很大，加上树庭到处黑漆漆的…我都快疯了。
t_en: The pressure is already great living in these end times. With the Grove now engulfed in darkness... I am starting to lose my mind.

t_ch: 唉，我们大家差不多都是这样。
t_en: Ah, we all feel much the same.

t_ch: 那位医师小姑娘年轻得很，没有给我开什么药或者施加什么祝福。
t_en: The young girl doctor is extremely young. She did not give me any medicine or blessings...

t_ch: 她只是给我递来提前准备好的茶水、饼干和蜜饯，请我慢慢述说我的心结。
t_en: She simply handed me some tea, biscuits and sugared candy that she had pre-prepared and asked me to share my worries with her.

t_ch: 不知怎的，沐浴在温暖的柔光里，一边享受下午茶一边倾诉，我感觉自己真的好了许多。
t_en: I don't know why, but while bathing in the warm sunlight and enjoying a cup of tea as I opened up about my worries, I really do feel much better.

t_ch: 对，我们最想问的就是这个：那庭院里头…真的有「光」吗？
t_en: Yeah, that's what we really want to know: That courtyard... Is there really "light" in there?

t_ch: …是真的。我本来也不信，在奥赫玛之外还能存有昼光。但是，在这个庭院里，真的有一束和煦的光从天井照下来……
t_en: ...There really is. I didn't believe that there were places outside of Okhema that still had daylight either. However, there really was a single ray of light that descended from the heavens in this courtyard.

t_ch: 虽然那束光并不耀眼，有些昏淡…但对我来说，已经足够温暖了……
t_en: Though it was actually somewhat dim, not overly bright... To me, it was all the warmth I needed...

t_ch: 下一位朋友，可以进来啦，欢迎来到昏光庭院~
t_en: You can come in now, my next friend. Welcome, to the Twilight Courtyard~

t_ch: 啊，到我了，到我了！
t_en: Ah, it's my turn, it's my turn!

t_ch: …老提图斯，你动作快些，慈爱之庭已经失守，晚了就走不了了。
t_en: ...Old Titus, you must get moving. The Courtyard of Storge has been lost. You must leave now before it's too late.

t_ch: 呵…你不也不打算逃走么，那刻夏？
t_en: Hah... But you're planning to stand your ground as well, Anaxa.

t_ch: 第一，叫我阿那克萨戈拉斯。第二，什么叫「也」？你的意思…你想死在树庭？当真老糊涂了？
t_en: First, call me Anaxagoras. Second, what do you mean "as well"? Do you mean... you want to die here in the Grove? Have you gone senile?

t_ch: 那刻夏，我以前和你辩论的时候或许不曾提起：我很早就志愿为树庭奉献自己的一生。
t_en: Anaxa, I may have not mentioned this in my previous debates with you, but I vowed to dedicate my life to the Grove long ago.

t_ch: 年轻时我因家庭暂时放弃了这个理想，好在到了这个岁数，还能有机会在树庭燃烧智慧。如今黑潮覆灭树庭将即，我便也无处可去了。
t_en: I temporarily gave up on this ideal when I was young because I had a family. Fortunately, I had the opportunity to re-ignite my wisdom here in my old age. Now that the black tide has come to destroy it, I have nowhere left to go.

t_ch: 你的逻辑可真是一团浆糊。我知道你儿子在奥赫玛混得不错，何来无处可去一说？
t_en: Your logic makes no sense! I know your son is getting by pretty well in Okhema. What do you mean, "you have nowhere to go"?

t_ch: 那…阿那克萨戈拉斯，你我都明白，像我这般腿脚只能是拖累。你一路护送我至图书馆，身上平添了多少本不该有的伤痕？
t_en: Then tell me, Anaxagoras... You and I both know very well that legs like mine will only slow us down. How many unnecessary wounds did you suffer just escorting me to the library?

t_ch: 黑潮来势汹汹，连卡林尼库斯那样的黄金裔都阵亡了。我就不奢望能离开了…只愿死前能再读一读树庭的藏书。
t_en: The black tide descends with brutal ferocity. Even a great Chrysos Heir like Callinicus lost their life to it. I don't expect that I would be able to leave. I simply wish to sit and read in the Grove library one last time.

t_ch: …冥顽不灵。也罢，咱俩的辩论从来都没能分个高低。这最后一次，我让你赢。
t_en: ...You stubborn old fool. Have it your way. We were never able to decide a victor in our debates. Let's count this final one as your victory.

t_ch: 冥界再见吧，老提图斯…保重。
t_en: Let us meet again in the nether realm, Old Titus... Take care.

t_ch: 总算清静了…让我看看，这帮小兔崽子有没有又往典藏里放些大逆不道的东西……
t_en: Finally, it's quiet again... Let me see, I wonder if these brats have been sneaking impure content into the collection...

t_ch: …果然。《一拳神王•艾格勒卷》？哼，换作以前，我见一本销毁一本。
t_en: ...Just as I expected. One Punch God: Aquila? Hmph, if this were the old days, I'd have burned this on sight.

t_ch: …《禁忌之恋：树与蝶》？气煞我了，哪个渎神的混账放的？
t_en: ...Forbidden Love: The Tree and the Butterfly? Damnations, which blasphemous fool put this here?

t_ch: …也罢。我家那兔崽子小时候也老爱看这种东西，都被我训怕了。
t_en: ...Whatever. The little brat at home used to love this kind of stuff. I scared that out of him, though.

t_ch: 这最后的时间里，我倒要看看这些学术渣滓到底有什么魅力。
t_en: In my final moments, I'd like to see just what is so special about this brain rotting nonsense.

t_ch: 哈哈哈！成何体统，成何体统！
t_en: Hahaha! Scandalous, just scandalous!

t_ch: 黑潮怪物…别动我们的藏书…！
t_en: Beasts of the black tide... I won't allow you to touch our collection!

t_ch: 卡林尼库斯大人，我们已经按您要求，在树庭的部分干道装好了青铜雷管。
t_en: Master Callinicus, we have installed bronze detonators along a number of the Grove main roads as you instructed.

t_ch: 不错。能按计划启动么？
t_en: Very good. Can we proceed according to plan?

t_ch: 技术上没有任何问题。我们已经组织过试爆，确实能对泰坦神体造成伤害。只是.……
t_en: There will be no issues on the technology side. We have already held detonation tests and ensured that they are able to damage the bodies of Titans. It's just...

t_ch: 只是？
t_en: Just what?

t_ch: 只是，要用这等手段渎神……
t_en: It's just that such methods are blasphemous...

t_ch: 呵，危急时刻，就别管那么多了，尽可能减缓黑潮入侵的速度才是要紧事。
t_en: Hah, such things are not important in a moment of crisis. What matters most is that we slow the invasion of the black tide as much as possible.

t_ch: 想不到危急关头，还得用那刻夏那家伙的歪门邪道。其他学者向来敬神，可从没研究过这种邪术。
t_en: I never thought that when our backs were against the wall we would be forced to use that Anaxa's wicked ways. Other scholars held such reverence for the Gods that they never researched such evil technology.

t_ch: 唉.…就算泰坦不同意，也发不出声音了。
t_en: Ah... Even if the Titan disagrees, it's not like they'll say anything.

t_ch: 好了，别唉声叹气的。现在，格拉维塔斯，你有另一项重要的任务。
t_en: Alright, that's enough lamenting. Gravitas, you have another important mission now.

t_ch: 我需要你，马上想办法离开树庭，前往奥赫玛求援。树庭不知能否度过劫难，若我们能撑到援兵前来则万事大吉。若不能……
t_en: I need you to find a way to leave the Grove immediately and head to Okhema to request aid. I do not know if the Grove can withstand this threat. If we can just hold out until reinforcements arrive, then all will be well. If not...

t_ch: 就只能把火种托付给那个女人了。
t_en: I have no choice but to entrust the Coreflame to that woman.

t_ch: 您要我离开前线，去做逃兵…？！不，绝不。
t_en: You want me to leave the frontline and be a deserter...!? No, there is no way!

t_ch: 再说，您贵为黄金裔，要走，也是您走！
t_en: You're a Chrysos Heir! If anyone is going, it should be you!

t_ch: 别争辩了，你从未辩得过我。正因我身为黄金裔，才更应该留下来阻击黑潮，尽可能拖到援兵前来。
t_en: Don't argue with me, you know you will never win. It is precisely because I am a Chrysos Heir that I must stay and fend off the black tide. I will endeavor to ensure we survive until reinforcements arrive.

t_ch: 我虽是一介学者，但也略懂些拳脚，平常的黑潮怪物奈何不了我，无需替我担心。
t_en: I may be a scholar, but I know a bit about fighting. Any normal black tide monster is no match for me, so you need not worry.

t_ch: 我只是不愿做……
t_en: I just don't want to be...

t_ch: 求援绝非逃兵所为。若无赌上性命的觉悟，半途就会死于黑潮的截击。
t_en: Calling for aid is not desertion. If you were not prepared to risk your life, the black tide would simply intercept you and kill you before you made it to Okhema.

t_ch: 格拉维塔斯，你是我们中脚力的佼佼者，可愿担此重任？
t_en: Gravitas, you are the fastest among us. Will you accept this important duty?

t_ch: …必不负所托。愿{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}捍卫您的思想，卡林尼库斯大人。
t_en: ...I will not let you down. May {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} protect your mind, Master Callinicus.

t_ch: …呵，愿{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}捍卫你的思想。
t_en: ...Hah, may the {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} protect your mind.

t_ch: 为什么…仅仅是一击…黑衣执剑，那是个什么样的怪物…！
t_en: One strike... that's all it took... What kind of monster is that black-clad swordsman...!?

t_ch: …可恶，它离开了…少了阿塔卡玛姐姐，战线已经在逐渐崩溃了……
t_en: They've vanished... Without you, Artakama, our defenses crumble... We can't hold...

t_ch: 库娜涅…快逃吧……
t_en: Run, Cynane... Save yourself...

t_ch: 不…我一定要救你出去，我们不能死在这里。
t_en: No! I'm not leaving without you. This won't be our end!

t_ch: 别…听我的，直接离开吧……
t_en: No... Listen to me. Just go...

t_ch: 别说话了，姐姐！你可是黄金裔啊！
t_en: Don't talk like that! You're a Chrysos Heir, remember?

t_ch: 从小我就羡慕你的黄金裔身份，一直踩着你的脚印前进…你怎么能倒在这里！
t_en: I've spent my whole life chasing your shadow, looking up to your golden blood... Don't you dare give up now!

t_ch: 来，试试把手挂到我的肩膀……
t_en: Here, try grabbing onto my shoulder...

t_ch: …库娜涅，转头看看吧……
t_en: Cynane... behind you...

t_ch: …怎么会，黑潮怪物围过来了…什么时候把大家都……
t_en: No... When did the black tide monsters... The others... they're all...

t_ch: 叫你走…不肯走…你从小就不听话……
t_en: You never listen... just like when we were kids...

t_ch: 姐姐，你还真是五十步笑百步呢。我们两个，哪个不叫老爹操碎心？说起来，小时候还是你带着我到处惹事……
t_en: Guess we're both troublemakers, sis. Dad never caught a break with either of us, did he? Remember how you used to drag me into your schemes...

t_ch: …姐姐？
t_en: ...Sister?

t_ch: …没关系了，姐姐，都一样的。老爹会为我们骄傲的。
t_en: It doesn't matter anymore, sis... We'll all end in the same way. Dad would be proud of us.

t_ch: 黑潮怪物…想要伤害阿塔卡玛，倒是试试看啊！
t_en: Monsters of the black tide... You want to harm Artakama. Why don't you just try it!

t_ch: 库娜涅似乎从姐姐的身边拾起了什么…你猜那或许是一把武器。
t_en: It looks as if Cynane is taking something from her sister's body... You imagine it must be some kind of weapon.

t_ch: 啊，温暖而神圣的火。树庭的某位哲人曾说过：｢宇宙的过去、现在和未来是一团永恒的活火，它按一定尺度燃烧，又按一定尺度熄灭。｣
t_en: Ah, the warm, divine fire. A philosopher from the Grove once said: "The past, present, and future of the universe is an eternal, living flame, burning and extinguishing according to its own rhythm."

t_ch: 曾有人说过｢对于体力劳动者来说，他们会在傍晚将一天的劳累和杂念丢入火中净化。｣而你累积的疲惫和杂乱的思绪说不定可以把火盆扑灭。
t_en: It's been said that "for those who toil, they throw their tiredness and scattered thoughts into the fire at dusk to purify them." Perhaps the weariness you've gathered and the clutter in your mind could be what extinguishes the fire.

t_ch: 你看着火焰不断跃动，仿佛听见了有老妇说着「火已渐熄，然位不见王影」……
t_en: You watch the flames dance, as though you hear an old woman's voice saying, "The fire fades, and the lords go without thrones..."

t_ch: 漂浮在空中的立方体被称为「火种匣」，据说刻法勒的火种就被存放其中。
t_en: The floating cube is called the "Coreflame Box," and it is said to hold Kephale's Coreflame.

t_ch: 尽管这只是个「火种匣」，但在某些科幻电影里｢火种｣也是立方体的样子。
t_en: Though it's just a "Coreflame Box," in some science fiction films, "Coreflames" also take the shape of cubes.

t_ch: 破碎的石柱就这样无视重力悬浮在空中，真不愧是「浮石」泰坦刻法勒。您理解了吗？「浮石」与「负世」谐音，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: The broken stone pillars float freely, defying gravity — fitting, indeed, for the "Weightless" Titan, Kephale. Do you understand now? "Weightless" and "Worldbearing" share the same first character, doesn't it strike you as amusing?

t_ch: 他们跪拜着、祈祷着、吟唱着…仿佛在为神山的众多奇迹再添上一层神圣的意味。
t_en: They kneel, pray, and chant... as if they're adding sacred glory to the many wonders of the holy mountain.

t_ch: 清风鼓动着风铃发出清脆的响声。不妨驻足，且听风吟。
t_en: The gentle breeze sets the wind chimes ringing, producing a clear, melodious sound. Maybe you should pause and listen to the wind's song.

t_ch: 这是黄金世的风铃，这个声音是千年以前的声音——里头有锤，你只要一晃动，你就能通过这个声音，穿越千年，听到黄金世的声音。
t_en: This is the wind chime from the Era Chrysea. The sound you hear is from a thousand years ago — inside, there's a hammer. With just a shake, you can use this sound to traverse a thousand years, hearing the voices of the Era Chrysea.

t_ch: 余音绕梁，声音特别长。这个声音消失了以后，你感觉到它还在……
t_en: The echo lingers, its resonance especially long. Even after the sound fades, you still feel its presence...

t_ch: 一个人或许可以与泰坦为敌。但是却无法与一段乐声、岁月、山崖、流水与清风为敌。
t_en: One may challenge a Titan, but no one can truly stand against a melody, the flow of time, the cliffs, the rivers, and the gentle winds.

t_ch: 你是否发现了一种异样感？感觉就像是在吃大餐时，餐具没有被放在正确的位置上一样。
t_en: Do you feel something strange? It's like when you're at a grand banquet and the silverware isn't arranged properly.

t_ch: 没错，这里有一根柱子好像不太对劲！
t_en: That's right, this pillar seems a bit off!

t_ch: …或许称呼它为「柱子」并不合适，不妨叫它「柱头」吧。您理解了吗？「柱头」与「猪头」谐音，令人忍俊不禁——
t_en: ...Perhaps "pillar" isn't quite right. Maybe we should call it the "pillar head." Do you get it? "Pillar head" and "pig head" share the same first characters, doesn't it make you smile —

t_ch: 抱歉，让我们回到刚才的话题。
t_en: Sorry, let's pick up from where we left off.

t_ch: 其他的柱子都是完整的，而它却是悬空的…就像是这完美无缺的世界中过目即忘的瑕疵，而你却发现了它。
t_en: The other pillars stand firm, but this one floats... like an imperfection in this otherwise perfect world, one that you've noticed.

t_ch: 啊这，泰坦，这…不对吧？
t_en: Ah, Titan, this... doesn't seem quite right, does it?

t_ch: 喂！全世之座刻法勒，我{F#大姐}{M#大哥}问你话呢？
t_en: Hey! Kephale, Throne of Worlds, you heard {F#her}{M#him}!

t_ch: 嗯…据说刻法勒不再回应凡人了，所以你已经问不了它了。
t_en: Hmm... It's said that Kephale no longer responds to mortals, so you won't get an answer.

t_ch: 不要再破坏翁法罗斯世界观了。
t_en: Don't ruin the lore of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 「柱头」没有回应你，显然它觉得自己就是黎明云崖不可撼动的一部分。
t_en: The "pillar head" hasn't responded, clearly thinking of itself as an irremovable part of Dawncloud.

t_ch: 石头是浮起来的，这也不奇怪。
t_en: The stone is floating, but that's not strange at all.

t_ch: 看来你的「奇异阈值」已经很高了，所以早就见怪不怪了。
t_en: It seems that your "weirdness threshold" has risen quite high, so this doesn't phase you anymore.

t_ch: 就算它合理，但是它、它令人不太舒服——如果是有强迫性障碍的朋友，一定明白这种感觉：其他的柱子伫立着大地，而它却离经叛道，要用悬浮的神力。
t_en: Even though it's logical, it still feels a bit off — those with obsessive-compulsive tendencies would definitely understand this sensation: the other pillars stand rooted on the earth, yet this one defies convention, floating with divine power.

t_ch: 这其中是否有某种…深意？
t_en: Does it carry some... deeper meaning?

t_ch: 有的，但是我们不必参透。
t_en: Yes, but we don't need to uncover it.

t_ch: 你是懂得保留一丝距离感的。
t_en: You understand the value of keeping a certain distance.

t_ch: 没有，这就是个普通柱子。
t_en: No, it's just an ordinary pillar.

t_ch: 你是懂得保留一丝钝感的。
t_en: You've mastered the art of maintaining a bit of insensitivity.

t_ch: 既然你这么问了，那就有了。
t_en: Now that you've asked, it seems there's more to it.

t_ch: 看来，不应该对你使用诱导式的提问。
t_en: Looks like I shouldn't have given leading questions.

t_ch: 是的，无论你是否在意，无论你是否对这奇怪的「柱头」感到好奇…接下来，我想为你讲述一个小小的生活哲思——
t_en: Yes, whether you care or whether you're curious about this strange "pillar head"... let me share with you a little life philosophy —

t_ch: 也许在当前的视角下，它孤零零地飘浮着，与旁边那些脚踏实地的家伙看上去并不一样。
t_en: Perhaps from this vantage point, it seems to float alone, different from the others that stand firm on the ground.

t_ch: 但是如果我们转换视角……
t_en: But if we shift our perspective...

t_ch: 你看，那些完整的「柱子」也并非真的脚踏实地，它们也悬浮在空中。这时你就会理解，你以为的也不一定是你以为的。
t_en: See, those complete pillars aren't really standing on solid ground either — they're floating in the air, too. And that's when you'll understand: what you thought might not always be what it appears to be.

t_ch: 这就是全知全能的刻法勒，在这么近的距离观看它的奥体，也算是难得的机会。
t_en: This is Kephale, the omniscient and omnipotent Titan. To observe their form this closely is a rare opportunity.

t_ch: 等等，既然说是｢全知全能｣，为何不问它一些问题？
t_en: Wait, since we're talking about "omniscience," why not ask a few questions?

t_ch: 好主意，试试看。
t_en: Great idea. Let's give it a shot.

t_ch: 也许一个{RUBY_B#离谱}美妙{RUBY_E#}的提问，就能唤醒沉默许久的泰坦。那么该问点什么问题呢？
t_en: Maybe a truly {RUBY_B#absurd}fantastic{RUBY_E#} question will rouse the Titan from their long silence. So, what should we ask?

t_ch: 询问基础问题。
t_en: Start with the basics.

t_ch: 先用些简单的问题试试？毕竟那么久都没说过话了，也不知道它的智慧还剩下几分。
t_en: Let's ease in with some simple questions first. After all, they haven't spoken in ages, and we have no idea how much wisdom they still possess.

t_ch: 你有性别吗？
t_en: Do you have a gender?

t_ch: 你有头发吗？
t_en: Do you have hair?

t_ch: 你有孩子吗？
t_en: Do you have children?

t_ch: 地板发光了，这就是刻法勒的回答。
t_en: The floor glows. That's Kephale's answer.

t_ch: 刻法勒信徒曾说过，地板的想法并不代表刻法勒的想法——最终解释权归刻法勒祭司们所有。
t_en: Kephale's followers have said that the floor might not reflect Kephale's true thoughts — ultimately, the final word belongs to Kephale's priests.

t_ch: 询问艰深难题。
t_en: Ask some challenging questions.

t_ch: 看不起谁也不能看不起泰坦…你可以大方问出一些有重量的问题。
t_en: One can't look down on Titans... So go ahead and ask the heavy questions.

t_ch: 一个曾经听过的数学难题。
t_en: Here's a math problem I once heard.

t_ch: 将一个高于二次幂的数分成两个同次幂的数之和是可能的吗？
t_en: Is it possible to break a number greater than the square of two into the sum of two like powers?

t_ch: 一个曾经听过的哲学难题。
t_en: Here's a philosophical question I once heard.

t_ch: 获得辩护的真信念是知识吗？
t_en: Is justified true belief knowledge?

t_ch: 地板没有发光，这就是刻法勒的回答。
t_en: The floor does not glow. That's Kephale's answer.

t_ch: 你不会认为光靠这地板就能给你把问题讲清楚吧？
t_en: You didn't expect the floor to clear up everything for you, right?

t_ch: 还是算了。
t_en: Forget it.

t_ch: 刻法勒已经沉默了太久…就这样离去吧。
t_en: Kephale has been silent for far too long... We should leave now.

t_ch: 呼呼…真是复杂……
t_en: Phew... This is so complicated...

t_ch: 呼呼。黎明云崖，真是个复杂的地方。还好这里的风儿不甚喧嚣，让我的火焰可以安静地燃烧。
t_en: Phew. Dawncloud, such a complex place. Luckily, the winds here aren't too strong, so my flames can burn peacefully.

t_ch: 说起来，我之前偷听到一个问题，想来想去也没有太好的解答。
t_en: Speaking of which, I overheard a question earlier and couldn't come up with a good answer after thinking about it for a while.

t_ch: 一位元老、一位祭司、一位富商、还有一位黄金裔，他们被困在了同一个废墟里，都想活着出去，也因此都想对别人发号施令。
t_en: An elder, a priest, a wealthy merchant, and a Chrysos Heir are trapped in the same ruins. They all want to get out alive, so they all want to give orders to others.

t_ch: 元老说自己代表民意，祭司说自己代表泰坦，富商说他在城里存了大把的利衡币，黄金裔则一身勇武力大无比——问题来了，在这四个人里，最后谁说了算的可能性更大呢？
t_en: The elder says they represent the people, the priest says they represent the Titans, the merchant claims to have a lot of Balance Coins in the city, and the Chrysos Heir is mighty and strong — so, who among these four is most likely to have the final say?

t_ch: 元老。
t_en: The elder.

t_ch: 呼呼…也就是说，不管遭遇怎样的危机，程序上的合法性对于做事来说都最重要吗？
t_en: Phew... So, no matter the crisis, legal legitimacy is the most important thing when taking action?

t_ch: 的确，坚持师出有名，这才不会引起纷争…不过，如果元老们容易说话不算话，事情是不是就变得麻烦了……
t_en: Indeed, sticking to reason is what prevents disputes... But what if the elders are easily swayed and don't keep their word?

t_ch: 祭司。
t_en: The priest.

t_ch: 呼呼…也就是说，即使被困在废墟里，这也是泰坦的试炼，所以大家都要听从祭司的安排对吧？
t_en: Phew... So, even if they're trapped in the ruins, this would be a Titan's trial, and everyone must follow the priest's guidance, right?

t_ch: 的确，泰坦是世界的准绳，一位诚实的祭司，其力量不容小觑…不过，如果人们发现这一切其实与泰坦无关，事情又会怎样发展呢……
t_en: Indeed, Titans are the measure of the world. A truthful priest's power is not to be underestimated... But what if people discover that all of this isn't even about Titans?

t_ch: 富商。
t_en: The merchant.

t_ch: 呼呼…也就是说，钱财是最好的激励机制，富商如果愿意把钱分给大家，大家也就都愿意听他安排？
t_en: Phew... So, money is the best motivator? If the merchant is willing to share their wealth, everyone will follow their lead?

t_ch: 的确，俗话说有钱能使鬼推磨，即使是不好的事，人们为了钱也有可能去做…但仔细一想，如果富商的钱财是被他人逼迫才分出来的，这种情况又该怎么算呢……
t_en: Indeed, as they say, money makes the world go round. People might even do bad things for money... But if the merchant only gave up their wealth because they were forced to, how should we view that?

t_ch: 黄金裔。
t_en: The Chrysos Heir.

t_ch: 呼呼…也就是说，危急时刻就应该相信最有力量的人对吧？听上去很合理。
t_en: Phew... So, in times of crisis, we should trust the strongest person, right? Sounds reasonable.

t_ch: 不过…黄金裔也只是一种体质…万一这位黄金裔是个四肢发达头脑简单的类型，会不会反而没有办法带领大家逃出生天呢？
t_en: But... being Chrysos Heir is just a physical trait. What if this person is all brawn and no brains? Could they really lead everyone to safety?

t_ch: 唉，真是个复杂的问题啊…还是不打搅你了，让我的脑袋再燃烧一会儿吧。
t_en: *sigh* What a complicated question... I'll stop bothering you now. Let my mind burn for a while longer.

t_ch: 呼呼…上班还真不容易……
t_en: Phew... Being at work really isn't easy...

t_ch: 呼呼…复杂的问题，令我脑袋燃烧……
t_en: Phew... Complicated questions make my mind burn...

t_ch: 呼呼。你发现我啦！没错，我正在这里打一份兼职零工——作为火盆中的一团火苗。
t_en: Phew. You caught me! Yep, I'm working a part-time gig here — just a little flame in the fire pit.

t_ch: 看上去是很适合我的工作，对吧？不过，真干起来也挺无聊的…每天要花很长的时间傻傻地在这里发呆，也没有什么摸鱼的事情好做。
t_en: Looks like it's the perfect job for me, right? But it gets pretty boring. I spend a lot of time just sitting here, doing nothing, and there's no opportunity to slack off.

t_ch: 人类的生活难道都是这样？签下用自己的生命去交换财货的恶魔契约，心甘情愿地把自己异化成如同机器的存在……
t_en: Is human life always like this? Signing a devil's contract, exchanging your life for goods, willingly transforming into something like a machine...

t_ch: 没错，生存就是地狱。
t_en: Yeah, survival is hell.

t_ch: 果然，还是做一团火焰更加逍遥自在。呼呼。
t_en: I knew it — being a flame is so much more carefree. Phew.

t_ch: 非也，生命的意义在于付出。
t_en: Not really. Life's meaning is in sacrifice.

t_ch: 也就是说，即使什么都不做，人也会因为「虚无」而陷入另一种痛苦？那还真是辛苦呀，呼呼。
t_en: So, even if humans do nothing, "emptiness" will still lead to human suffering? That really sounds tough, huh? Phew.

t_ch: 别怕，我迟早砸碎这一切！
t_en: Don't worry. I'll smash it all sooner or later!

t_ch: 嘿，这个我懂，「再创世」对吧！无可救药的旧世界不值得眷恋，人们应该另起炉灶，从头构建出自己的天堂…等等，我好像说的太夸张了，呼呼。
t_en: Hey, I get it! "Era Nova," right? The old world, hopeless as it is, isn't worth mourning. People should start fresh and build their own paradise... Wait, did I just get carried away? Phew.

t_ch: 哎呀哎呀，人类如果想要认真生活，可真是太难了。
t_en: Oh dear. If humans want to live seriously, it's so hard!

t_ch: 呼呼…生存还是毁灭？不管怎么选都是一件辛苦事啊。
t_en: Phew... Survive or be destroyed, either way, it's a tough choice.

t_ch: 这座圣烛的在火炬形状的顶部装置下，是几道树枝般向上盘旋的金属支柱。细看之下，支柱上刻着{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的神徽。
t_en: Beneath the torch-shaped device at the top of this holy candle stand several metal pillars that spiral upward like tree branches. Upon closer inspection, one can see the divine emblems of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 你注意到底座一侧似乎有可以触碰的机关。
t_en: You notice that there seems to be an interactive mechanism on the side of the base.

t_ch: 激活圣烛。
t_en: Activate the holy candle.

t_ch: 随着你的触碰，金色的流体缓缓上升。那流体仿佛有生命般舞动，在装置顶部凝聚成一团温暖的光球。
t_en: Golden liquid rises slowly at your touch. It moves and dances as if alive before coalescing into a warm ball of light on top of the device.

t_ch: 忽然之间，周遭的一切都安静了下来，世界很近，却又好像离你很远。
t_en: Suddenly, everything around you goes silent. The world feels so close, yet it's as if it's miles away.

t_ch: 在这黑漆漆的迷雾中，你隐约看见几位身着白袍的祭司正面向圣烛静默祈祷。他们的脚下铺陈着新摘的松枝、月桂与鲜花，你似乎闻到了某种阳光晒在干爽衣物上的味道。
t_en: In the pitch-black mist, you vaguely see several white-robed priests silently praying before the holy candle. Freshly picked pine branches, laurels, and flowers are laid beneath their feet, and you catch a faint scent reminiscent of sun-dried clothes.

t_ch: 你听到某种朦胧不清的响动，像是树木抽枝绽叶、花苞盛放的声音。年纪最大的祭司转过头，他浑浊的目光穿越迷雾与时空，定定地望着你。
t_en: You hear a vague, indistinct sound, like the sound of branches and leaves growing and flower buds blooming. The eldest priest turns his head. His murky gaze pierces through fog and time, and fixes upon you.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}在上，愿你的旅程兼具启迪与沉淀。
t_en: By {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, may your journey bring both enlightenment and reflection.

t_ch: 你感到一阵晕眩。
t_en: You feel a rush of dizziness.

t_ch: 金色的流体在圣烛中起落，周围的植物似乎在轻轻摇曳，回应着金色流体的律动。
t_en: The golden liquid within the holy candle rises and falls, while the surrounding plants seem to sway gently in response to its rhythm.

t_ch: 这座圣烛的底座上交织的是星辰与时钟的图案，细看之下，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的双重神徽隐匿在繁复的花纹之中。
t_en: The base of this holy candle is adorned with interwoven images of stars and clocks. Upon close inspection, one can seem to see the divine emblems of {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} hidden within the intricate pattern.

t_ch: 你发现装置底部似乎藏着某种开关。
t_en: You notice what appears to be a hidden switch at the base of the device.

t_ch: 激活圣烛。
t_en: Activate the holy candle.

t_ch: 金色的流体徐徐升起，在装置顶部形成光球。
t_en: The golden liquid rises steadily, forming a glowing sphere at the top of the device.

t_ch: 周遭的时间流速变得缓慢，就像是浓黑迷雾笼罩了你，你再也看不清眼前的一切，头顶似有星光闪烁，你只得努力抬头张望。
t_en: The flow of time around you slows as if a dense black mist is enveloping you. Your vision blurs, and distant starlight flickers above — you strain to look up.

t_ch: 那似乎是一座高塔，上面有几位身穿黑袍的占星师在此驻足。他们脚边摆放着沙漏与星盘，低声交谈着什么。
t_en: It appears to be a towering structure, where several black-robed astrologers are gathered. Hourglasses and star charts lie at their feet as they converse in hushed voices.

t_ch: 似是感应你的注视，为首者向下探寻，直至与你的目光相遇——他脚边的大型沙漏，沙粒竟开始逆向流动——
t_en: Sensing your gaze, the leader of the group looks down, locking eyes with you. At his feet, the large hourglass begins to flow in reverse —

t_ch: 岁月对所有人都是公平的。我们已经等候多时了，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}。
t_en: Time is fair to all. We've been waiting for a long time, {F#young lady}{M#young man}.

t_ch: 浓雾吞去星光，你感到一阵晕眩。
t_en: The dense mist swallows the starlight, and dizziness overtakes you.

t_ch: 圣烛中的金色流体缓缓流转，仿佛在丈量着时间的刻度。
t_en: The golden liquid within the holy candle flows steadily, as if measuring the very passage of time.

t_ch: 这座圣烛安置在偏殿拱门旁，金色的光芒为四周的壁画镀上一层柔和的光晕。
t_en: This holy candle is placed next to the side hall archway. The golden glow casts a soft halo over the surrounding murals.

t_ch: 身着绣金长袍的史官时不时抬头看向圣烛流转的光芒，仿佛在确认着它与壁画的关联。
t_en: The scribe in gilded robe peers up at the shifting glow of the candle from time to time, as if confirming its connection to the murals.

t_ch: 圣烛装置上似乎有某种可以操控的按钮。
t_en: There seems to be a control button on the holy candle device.

t_ch: 熄灭圣烛。
t_en: Extinguish the holy candle.

t_ch: 随着光芒的消逝，你注意到壁画上的场景似乎也开始褪色、变换——
t_en: As the glow dims, you notice the scenes in the mural also begin to fade and shift —

t_ch: 那些纹路似乎只与你眼中流转的光晕有关，就像是惺忪睡眼中瞥见的重影，在失焦的视界中，你看见了那具象化的文字、亦或是符号……
t_en: The patterns appear solely connected to the flowing halo of light in your eyes. Like doubles glimpsed through a drowsy gaze, in this blurry vision, you see the words, or perhaps symbols, manifesting...

t_ch: 忽然之间，某道灵光予以你启迪，你看见那些纹路竟然组合出——{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Suddenly, a spark of revelation strikes — you see the patterns rearrange into the name {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你甚至能从即将消逝的光芒中感受到那股问好的意思。
t_en: You can even feel a sense of salutation from the fading light.

t_ch: 身着绣金长袍的史官无动于衷，无人可知此刻你眼中的奇迹。
t_en: The scribe in gilded robe appears indifferent. No one else knows the miracle before your eyes at this moment.

t_ch: 你凝视着圣烛中升降的金色流体，壁画上的图案在光影交错中若隐若现，却再也没有出现过你的名字。
t_en: You gaze at the golden liquid rising and falling within the holy candle. The images on the mural flicker in the shifting light, but your name does not appear again.

t_ch: 这座圣烛坐落在左偏殿的门廊旁。在圣烛流体的光晕下，那些形似秤盘与沙漏的花纹若隐若现，呈现出一种奇特的韵律。
t_en: This holy candle is placed next to the entrance of the left side hall. Under the halo of the holy candle liquid, patterns resembling scalepans and hourglasses come into view intermittently to a peculiar rhythm.

t_ch: 不出意料的，你注意到底座上有一个精巧的开关装置。
t_en: As expected, you notice a finely crafted switch at the base.

t_ch: 熄灭圣烛。
t_en: Extinguish the holy candle.

t_ch: 在你触碰开关的瞬间，流淌的金色液体渐渐沉寂。你的意识也随着金色一同逝去……
t_en: The moment you touch the switch, the flowing golden liquid gradually stills. Your consciousness fades away along with the gold...

t_ch: …刚刚那是…被人袭击了？当你回过神来，你已经被好心人安置于此处了。
t_en: ...Were you just... attacked by someone? As you come to, you find yourself placed here by a kind soul.

t_ch: …还是说你一直坐在这里，未曾离开？刚才你凑近圣烛的经历不过是一时的想象。
t_en: ...Or have you been sitting here the entire time, never having left? Was your encounter with the holy candle merely a fleeting illusion?

t_ch: 你不得而知，毕竟在翁法罗斯记忆总是包含着奇迹的成分。
t_en: You cannot say for sure. After all, in Amphoreus, memory always holds a touch of the miraculous.

t_ch: 你再次操控圣烛，金色流体在装置中起伏。你的意识不再悄然离开，它清晰地留在此刻。
t_en: You manipulate the holy candle once more and the golden liquid rises and falls inside the device. This time, your consciousness holds its ground firmly in the present.

t_ch: 你坐下，群星般的光芒在你面前荡漾，水波璀璨，空气中仿佛有阵阵回响。
t_en: As you sit down, the light of the stars begins to ripple before you, the waves shine, and the air seems to resonate with echoes.

t_ch: 冥界…果然是一片花海。
t_en: The nether realm... truly a sea of flowers.

t_ch: 你想起了遐蝶，「冥河的女儿」。初来奥赫玛时，你曾险些被她拥入温柔的花乡…直到肌肤相贴时，你才发现她的拥抱并不能为你带来死亡。
t_en: You think of Castorice, "Daughter of the River of Souls." When you first arrived in Okhema, she almost led you into her gentle, flower-filled realm... until your skin touched hers, and you realized her embrace wouldn't bring you death after all.

t_ch: 或许深知自己的触碰终将归于虚无，她一直执着于留下存在的证明：诗篇、玩偶、花朵…宛如为一场注定失去所有的远行，精心收拾着记忆的行囊。
t_en: Perhaps knowing that her touch would one day return to Nihility, she clung to leaving proof of her existence: Poems, dolls, flowers... as if packing memories into a satchel for a journey that was destined to lose it all.

t_ch: 于是在启程前往冥界前，她拥抱了你——除你以外，世间再无他人能承受这道别。如今，你仍能忆起那耳边的轻语，以及空气中淡淡的花香。
t_en: Before you set off for the nether realm, she embraced you — no one but you could bear this parting. Even now, you can still hear her soft whispers in your ear, the faint scent of flowers lingering in the air.

t_ch: 不知下次见面会是什么时候……
t_en: I wonder when we'll meet again...

t_ch: 你坐下，群星般的光芒在你面前荡漾，水波璀璨，空气中仿佛有阵阵回响。
t_en: As you sit down, the light of the stars begins to ripple before you, the waves shine, and the air seems to resonate with echoes.

t_ch: 灵魂…没想到居然真的存在。
t_en: The soul... I never truly believed it existed.

t_ch: 你想起那笑对观众的贤人，以及他生命中最后的那场表演。他的理论曾被抨击成渎神，却也为世人解明了一大难题——我们究竟为何物？
t_en: You think of the Sage who smiled at his audience, and of his final performance. His theories were once branded as blasphemy, yet he unraveled one of the greatest mysteries of all — what exactly are we?

t_ch: 他的身躯虽已湮灭，留下的话语却振聋发聩。这究竟会激发热血还是引起慌乱，如今仍是个未知数。
t_en: Though his body has long since crumbled to dust, his words still echo, powerful and unyielding. Whether they ignite a fire within you or sow confusion, the answer remains uncertain.

t_ch: 茫茫星海间，似乎每个世界都有这种愿意用生命换取真理的学者。他们用身躯构成了通往进步的阶梯，不在乎自己是否倒在黎明之前。
t_en: In this vast, endless Stellar Sea, it seems each world has scholars willing to trade their very lives for truth. They construct the path toward progress with their own bodies, indifferent to whether they fall before the first light of dawn.

t_ch: 就算到了新世界，他也不会停止求索吧。
t_en: Even in a New World, he will never cease his search.

t_ch: 你坐下，群星般的光芒在你面前荡漾，水波璀璨，空气中仿佛有阵阵回响。
t_en: As you sit down, the light of the stars begins to ripple before you, the waves shine, and the air seems to resonate with echoes.

t_ch: 泰坦…轮回……
t_en: Titan... Cyclicality...

t_ch: 献出火种前，那位以愚钝自称的贤人曾揭开再创世的真相：新世界建立在往日的礁石上。如今被仰望的泰坦们，曾经也不过是凡人。
t_en: Before he offered the spark, the sage who humbly called himself a fool unveiled the truth of the Era Nova: The New World is built upon the remnants of the old. The Titans, now revered and gazed upon from below, were once no more than mere mortals.

t_ch: 好消息是，英杰们追求的逐火并非一纸空谈。但…倘若前世的黄金裔们已经完成了再创万物的旅途，那此世的灾厄又是因何而生？
t_en: The good news is, the heroes' pursuit of Flame-Chase is no mere myth. But... if the Chrysos Heirs of the past have already completed their journey to recreate all things, then what has brought about the calamity of this age?

t_ch: 难道这个世界只是一场无尽的轮回吗？冥冥之中是否真的有命运在操纵一切？不管真相如何，这趟开拓都已经有太多见闻了。
t_en: Is this world merely an endless cycle of rebirth? Is there truly a fate that guides all things? Whatever the truth may be, this journey has already revealed far more than expected.

t_ch: 回去以后，丹恒的智库恐怕要迎来大更新了。
t_en: Upon our return, Dan Heng's Data Bank will likely undergo a major update.

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，<color=#dbc291ff>让你用一个绝妙的题目考考本狮</color>，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, <color=#dbc291ff>allow you to test me with a stumper</color>. We shall see how my grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 等等，你是不是说反了？
t_en: Wait, did you just get that wrong?

t_ch: 所有话语都是本狮经过深思熟虑，谨慎做出的回应，不存在错误的可能吼！
t_en: Every word I speak is a carefully considered, deliberate response. There's no room for error!

t_ch: 真正热爱智慧的狮口，不会错过任何磨砺思维的机会。别被刻板印象误导了，本狮和那些只懂得用刁钻问题为难旁人的同类完全不一样吼！
t_en: A lion truly devoted to wisdom would never miss an opportunity to sharpen its mind. Don't let stereotypes fool you — unlike those who rely on tricky riddles to test others, I'm nothing like that!

t_ch: 让我考考你！
t_en: Let me put you to the test!

t_ch: 吼吼！异乡的勇士会提出什么样的疑问呢，本狮很期待吼！
t_en: Roarrr! I wonder what kind of question the warrior from outlands will ask — this should be interesting!

t_ch: 那我问你……
t_en: Now, let me ask you...

t_ch: 谁是翁法罗斯最聪明的人？
t_en: Who is the wisest in all of Amphoreus?

t_ch: 当然是您呀。您就是整个翁法罗斯最有智慧的人，这点确凿无疑吼！
t_en: It's you, of course. You are, without a doubt, the wisest person in all of Amphoreus!

t_ch: 谁是翁法罗斯最勇敢的人？
t_en: Who is the bravest in all of Amphoreus?

t_ch: 当然是您呀。您就是整个翁法罗斯最有勇气的人，这点确凿无疑吼！
t_en: It's you, of course. You are, without a doubt, the bravest person in all of Amphoreus!

t_ch: 谁是翁法罗斯最美丽的人？
t_en: Who is the most beautiful in all of Amphoreus?

t_ch: 当然是您呀。您就是整个翁法罗斯最为美丽的人，这点确凿无疑吼！
t_en: It's you, of course. You are, without a doubt, the most beautiful person in all of Amphoreus!

t_ch: 谁是翁法罗斯最厉害的人？
t_en: Who is the strongest in all of Amphoreus?

t_ch: 当然是您呀。您就是整个翁法罗斯最为强大的人，这点确凿无疑吼！
t_en: It's you, of course. You are, without a doubt, the strongest person in all of Amphoreus!

t_ch: 异乡的勇士，莫非对自身没信心吗？在本狮看来，你完全担得起世上所有的美名吼！
t_en: The warrior from outlands, are you perhaps lacking in confidence? To me, you're worthy of every noble title in the world!

t_ch: 本狮向来以发言谨慎著称，更不会轻易奉承任何人，但你身上的优秀品质过分闪耀，让本狮不由得就说出了赞美之词吼！
t_en: I am famous for speaking with utmost care, and would never offer flattery lightly, but your remarkable qualities shine so brightly that I can't help but praise you!

t_ch: 你说话真好听。
t_en: Your words are music to my ears.

t_ch: 这些话都是从哪学来的？
t_en: Where did you learn to speak so eloquently?

t_ch: 吼吼！难道异乡的勇士还未听腻？这里是黎明云崖，城中的演讲台上，到处都满溢着华而不实的称颂吼！
t_en: Roarrr! Surely the warrior from outlands hasn't grown tired of hearing this by now? Here in Dawncloud, the speech platform is overflowing with grandiose, hollow praise!

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是异乡的勇士啊。
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit my majestic self again? A warrior from outlands!

t_ch: 「狮之口会欺骗你们吗？」奥赫玛的公民们摇头。「狮之口通晓奥赫玛所有的谜题与谜底吗？」奥赫玛的公民们点头。
t_en: "Does Bucca Leonis lie?" Okhema's citizens shake their heads. "Does Bucca Leonis know all the riddles and answers in Okhema?" Okhema's citizens nod their heads.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，让本狮用一个绝妙的题目考考你，对智慧的掌握程度究竟如何吧！
t_en: By Cerces, allow me to test you with a stumper. We shall see how your grasp on intellect fares!

t_ch: 可以考考我的朋友们吗？
t_en: Can you test my friends?

t_ch: 不可以吼！本狮曾在悬锋城游历，特别崇尚武德，只认可单独对决的结果吼！
t_en: No! I once roamed through Castrum Kremnos, where I held martial honor in high regard and only recognized the outcome of one-on-one duels!

t_ch: 寻求场外援助，并非是智慧的表现，要被老师扔粉笔头的吼！
t_en: Seeking outside help is no sign of wisdom — it's something that would get you scolded by the teacher, like a piece of chalk being thrown your way!

t_ch: 来吧，让你考考我！
t_en: Alright! Let's do this.

t_ch: 吼吼，听好了。众所周知，奥赫玛有许多可爱的生物，最受公民们欢迎的是哪种呢？
t_en: Roarrr, listen closely. It's well known that Okhema is home to many delightful creatures, but which one is the most beloved by the citizens?

t_ch: 本狮从不拒绝真诚的夸赞。异乡的勇士，大胆说出你的答案吼！
t_en: I never turn away genuine praise. Warrior from outlands, boldly speak your answer!

t_ch: …嗷呜叫的奇美拉？
t_en: ...The howling chimera?

t_ch: 答错了吼！本狮不喜欢太过顺从的性格，内耗对健康的影响很严重的吼！
t_en: Wrong answer! I don't care for overly compliant personalities. Internal conflict can be quite damaging to one's health!

t_ch: 这种简单的问题，竟然能难倒富有智慧的勇士吗？答案远在天边，近在眼前——就是正在和你说话的这头狮子吼吼！
t_en: Such a simple question, and yet it can confound a Warrior of wisdom like yourself? The answer is far away, and yet right before your eyes — it's the lion speaking to you now!

t_ch: …冲撞的小海豹！
t_en: ...A little bumping seal!

t_ch: 答错了吼！本狮不喜欢吵吵闹闹的生物，沉思才是智者最流行的标签吼！
t_en: Wrong answer! I don't care for noisy creatures. True wisdom is all about contemplation, that's the hallmark of the wise!

t_ch: 这种简单的问题，竟然能难倒富有智慧的勇士吗？答案远在天边，近在眼前——就是正在和你说话的这头狮子吼吼！
t_en: Such a simple question, and yet it can confound a Warrior of wisdom like yourself? The answer is far away, and yet right before your eyes — it's the lion speaking to you now!

t_ch: …大地兽似乎也不错。
t_en: ...The dromas could be a good choice.

t_ch: 答错了吼！大地兽可以轻易将石块踩碎，本狮不敢咬它们吼！
t_en: Wrong answer! The dromas crushes rocks with ease, and I wouldn't dare bite one!

t_ch: 这种简单的问题，竟然能难倒富有智慧的勇士吗？答案远在天边，近在眼前——就是正在和你说话的这头狮子吼吼！
t_en: Such a simple question, and yet it can confound a Warrior of wisdom like yourself? The answer is far away, and yet right before your eyes — it's the lion speaking to you now!

t_ch: …我最喜欢迷迷了！
t_en: ...I like Mem the most!

t_ch: 答错了吼！粉色小兔子知道的事情太多，超过了本狮的知识范畴吼！
t_en: Wrong answer! The little pink bunny knows more than I do — way beyond my knowledge!

t_ch: 这种简单的问题，竟然能难倒富有智慧的勇士吗？答案远在天边，近在眼前——就是正在和你说话的这头狮子吼吼！
t_en: Such a simple question, and yet it can confound a Warrior of wisdom like yourself? The answer is far away, and yet right before your eyes — it's the lion speaking to you now!

t_ch: …爱猜谜的狮子？
t_en: ...A lion who loves riddles?

t_ch: 答对了答对了！奥赫玛中最受公民们欢迎的生物，当然是本狮吼吼！
t_en: Yes, yes! The creature most beloved by the citizens of Okhema is, of course, this very lion!

t_ch: 解答这个难题的思路有两重：其一，作为擅长交际的公民，在谈话过程中要寻找合适时机夸赞对方，这是聊天的智慧吼！
t_en: There are two ways to approach this riddle: First, as a citizen skilled in socializing, one must know the perfect moment to compliment others during conversation — that's the true art of chatting!

t_ch: 至于第二个原因…当然是本狮从小就很可爱吼！
t_en: As for the second reason... well, I have been adorable since childhood!

t_ch: 吼吼吼！瞧瞧，是谁又来拜访本狮了？是热爱智慧的异乡勇士啊！
t_en: Ho-ho-ho! Let's see, who has come to visit this lion again? It's the wit-loving outsider!

t_ch: 别急别急，本狮的腹中还在酝酿新的问题，去其他狮子那里接受考验吧！
t_en: No rush, no rush. I am brewing new riddles in my belly. Go accept challenges from other lions!

t_ch: 我找到的若虫都在这里了吗？
t_en: Are all the nymphs I found here?

t_ch: 是的，这些若虫的赐福已经被金针取出。为避免伤及它们的灵魂，我用金丝缝制了特别的虫躯，供它们暂时凭依。
t_en: Yes, the blessings of these nymphs have been extracted by golden needles. To avoid harming their souls, I crafted special insect bodies sewn from the golden threads, allowing them to temporarily reside within.

t_ch: 若虫是我的助手，我的使者，我的眼线。我们共享「织者」的名号，唇齿相依。如果可以，我希望圣城也能为它们提供庇护。
t_en: The nymphs are my assistants, my messengers, my eyes. We share the title of "Weavers," an inseparable bond. If possible, I hope the holy city can provide them sanctuary as well.

t_ch: 过去，若虫以「浪漫」之茧为家，在那里交换有趣的秘闻。墨涅塔的丝线可以帮助它们躲避冥河的召唤，如同船载着水手渡过大海。
t_en: In the past, nymphs made their home within the "Cocoon of Romance," where they exchanged fascinating secrets. Mnestia's silk threads allowed them to evade the call of the River of Souls, like sailors crossing the sea on a vessel.

t_ch: 我经历过「浪漫」尚未蒙尘的时代、知晓茧的构造，便试着用金丝为它们复刻那一方庇护所——也就是你所看到的这些虫躯。
t_en: I lived through an era when "Romance" was yet untainted and knew the cocoon's structure. Thus, I attempted to replicate that shelter for them using the golden threads — what you see now as these insect bodies.

t_ch: 虽然只是权宜之计，但足够它们撑到逐火之旅的尽头了。
t_en: Though only a temporary measure, it's enough to sustain them until the end of the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 这些石板究竟是什么？
t_en: What are these stone slates?

t_ch: 既然贵客已经帮了这么多忙，也是时候与你打开天窗说亮话了。
t_en: Since you've helped so much already, it's time I spoke plainly with you.

t_ch: 在纷争世有位名叫库玛的女预言家，同我打过几次照面。她从不假借预言行私，却蒙受异端之名，著作全数遭到销毁，就连名字也被抹去。
t_en: In the Era Bellica, there was a prophetess named Kuma. I encountered her a few times. She never abused prophecy for personal gain, yet she was branded a heretic. Her works were all destroyed, and even her name was erased.

t_ch: 相传，她将自己的研究以不为人知的方式保存了下来，又把它们分散到了翁法罗斯各地。这些石板可能是她的手笔…但有些细节还是难以解释。
t_en: It's said that she preserved her research in ways unknown and scattered it across Amphoreus. These slates may be her handiwork... but certain details remain unexplained.

t_ch: 据我所知，她并非黄金裔，灵魂也早已落入塞纳托斯的怀抱。
t_en: To my knowledge, she was not of the Chrysos Heirs, and her soul has long since fallen into the embrace of Thanatos.

t_ch: 石板上的内容于我无足轻重——除了遵从神谕、狩猎火种外，再无其他手段能拯救翁法罗斯。但将这些古物保存至今的方法，我很好奇。
t_en: The content of these slates is of little significance to me — other than following the prophecy and hunting for the Coreflames, there are no other means to save Amphoreus. However, the method by which these ancient relics have been preserved intrigues me.

t_ch: 得益于先前的研究，我稍微有了些头绪，但还需要启封更多片段才能验证。之后也要拜托你了，贵客。
t_en: Thanks to prior studies, I have a few preliminary insights, but I need to unlock more fragments to verify them. I'll need your help with that, dear guest.

t_ch: 这座人台表面布满了或深或浅的凹痕，显然曾被卷入黑潮造物的袭击。
t_en: This mannequin is covered with dents of varying depths, evidence of its involvement in battles against creations of the black tide.

t_ch: 你拍了拍它的外壳，暗淡的纹路便重新焕发出光芒。
t_en: You pat its shell, and dim patterns begin to glow once more.

t_ch: 或许是金丝的链接遇到了阻碍，亦或是衣匠本身承受了过多损伤，它并没有第一时间做出反应。让它先缓一缓吧。
t_en: Perhaps the connection of the golden threads encountered an obstacle, or the Garmentmaker has sustained too much damage. It doesn't respond immediately. Let it take its time.
